title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Transfer Challenge
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
Model direction and change with signed numbers and explain each operation on a number line.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 20 units, changes by −7 units, then by +4 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Rounding Precision for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation.
steps.why: The Rounding Precision result for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.
